title: Efficient extraction of high molecular weight dsDNA from bacteriophage (QIAGEN silica-based membrane method)
author: Mitchell G Hedges

steps_raw.instruction: DNase & RNase Digestion Master Mix Calculate the required volume of Digestion Mix for the number of samples you wish to process Each 450 µL sample of bacteriophage lysate requires 50 µL of Digestion Mix Remove DNase I, DNase 10x buffer and RNase A from storage and place On ice to thaw Gently pipette mix each tube 5 to 10 times. Do not vortex DNase I as it is sensitive to physical denaturation To a clean microcentrifuge tube, add 5 µL of DNAse I and 5 µL of RNAse A to 40 µL of 10x DNAse I reaction buffer, store On ice until required
DNEasy Blood & Tissue Kit Buffer Preparation Prepare kits buffers Buffer AW1 and Buffer AW2 with 100% molecular grade ethanol as per the manufacturer's manual
Add 50 µL of Digestion Master Mix to 450 µL of syringe filtered 0.22 µm bacteriophage lysate in a 1.5mL LoBind microtube and mix via gentle pipetting

Incubate for 00:30:00 at 37 °C using a Thermomixer without shaking Note: longer incubation times may be necessary to achieve complete degradation of bacterial DNA and RNA. It is advisable to quantify the amount of bacterial DNA and RNA pre- and post-digestion to ensure bacterial nucleic acids do not contaminate phage DNA. The volume of enzyme prescribed significantly degrades host DNA and RNA and has been demonstrated to facilitate whole genome sequencing without host DNA contamination. Assessing the DNA quality of both untreated lysate and treated lysate using agarose gel electrophoresis can be useful to determine the host nucleic acid sizes in your samples, and whether a post-extraction SPRI bead clean up may be required (described elsewhere).
Vortex the tube for 15 seconds and spin down tubes

Add 360 µL of ethanol (96 – 100%) and mix again thoroughly by vortexing It is essential that the sample, Buffer AL, and ethanol are mixed immediately and thoroughly by vortexing or pipetting to yield a homogeneous solution
Add 25 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA (final concentration >20 mM) to inactivate the DNase and incubate for 00:01:00 at 37 °C after gently pipette mixing

Incubate for 80 °C for 00:15:00 to heat inactivate DNase. Chill the tube at 4 °C for 00:01:00 and spin down tubes briefly to collect condensation. Hot tubes may pop open, so take care to avoid cross contamination
Add 20 µL proteinase K and 180 µL Buffer ATL to the sample, and mix by vortexing, incubate for 00:15:00 at 56 °C . Total volume of sample is now 720 µL
To each tube containing the lysis mixture, add 360 µL of Buffer AL and mix thoroughly by vortexing
Aliquot 360 µL of the mixture into a second microcentrifuge tube (ensure it is labelled) to split the sample which allows processing of the volumes in subsequent steps

Optional (recommended): Quantify the extracted DNA using a 1x dsDNA High Sensitivity Kit and a sample volume of 2 µL . Following favourable quantification, load between 5 ng to 20 ng of phage DNA with a loading dye onto a 1% TAE agarose gel prepared with 1X SYBR Safe Stain and run at 100V for 01:00:00 to assess DNA quality. Ensure a ladder such as 1kb Extend DNA Ladder (NEB) is used and loaded at the same mass as the phage DNA sample for visualisation on a trans-UV system such as ChemiDoc
Incubate at 56 °C for 00:05:00
Pipette 600 µL of the phage lysate mixture into a labelled silica-membrane spin column

Centrifuge the viral spin column in the collection tube at 6000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 , discarding the throughflow into a waste container. The throughflow is not compatible with bleach . Discard the tube to avoid liquid cross contamination
Repeat the above two steps until all phage sample has passed through the column
Place the silica-membrane spin column in a new collection tube, discard any used collection tubes and any throughflow
Add 500 µL of ethanol Wash Buffer 1 (AW1) onto the spin column
Centrifuge the spin column in the collection tube at 6000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00
Place the spin column in a new and clean collection tube. Discard the throughflow and do not combine with bleach . Discard the used collection tube
Add 500 µL of Wash Buffer 2 (AW2) into the spin column
Centrifuge the spin column in the collection tube at maximum speed 16000 x g, °C, to 20000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00

Discard the throughflow (this is compatible with bleach if required by laboratory processes) and replace the column back into the collection tube and spin at maximum speed 16000 x g, °C, to 20000 x g, °C, for 00:03:00 to dry the column

Place the spin column in a labelled, clean 1.5 ml LoBind tube (which will become the final sample tube for storage) and pipette 40 µL of warmed ( 56 °C ) Buffer AE directly onto the membrane. Incubate at 56 °C for 00:01:00 and then centrifuge for 00:01:00 at ≥ 6000 x g, °C, to elute
Re-elute the sample by pipetting the eluate back onto the silica membrane and incubate at 56 °C for 00:01:00 then centrifuge for 00:01:00 at ≥ 6000 x g, °C, to re-elute
Store the eluate containing the phage DNA at 2 - 8 ℃ in a labelled tube and take forward to DNA QA/QC processes / whole genome sequencing
